mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly reducing present equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS rises.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in > 60% of triggered instances or signal loses predictive power when UNRATE remains below 3.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real personal income growth outpacing consumer price inflation expands real household purchasing power, which directly increases corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative returns in > 50% of cases when RSAFS growth exceeds CPIAUCSL growth over consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the index of industrial production indicates weakening manufacturing output that reduces near-term corporate earnings revisions, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal fails to predict negative returns when INDPRO declines during periods of falling interest rates or positive earnings surprises.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts dropping below 1.4 million units annualized signals a contraction in construction activity and related consumer durables demand, which reduces forward earnings estimates and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive forward returns occur in > 55% of instances when HOUST crosses below the threshold during inventory restocking phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms broad-based labor market tightening that supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative returns when PAYEMS growth exceeds 200k but UNRATE remains flat or rises due to labor force participation shifts.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.0%, elevated borrowing costs compress equity valuations more aggressively than inflation hedging benefits, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Returns are positive in > 60% of triggered cases or the signal reverses direction when CPIAUCSL growth falls below 2.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects expanding aggregate economic activity that increases corporate profit margins and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows or GDPC1 growth fails to correlate with positive returns during recessionary periods.
